text: Delhi (Hindi: दिल्ली Dillī, Urdu: دلّی Dillī, Punjabi: ਦਿੱਲੀ Dilī) is one of India's largest cities and the core of the National Capital Territory of Delhi (NCT), with over 30 million inhabitants in its metropolitan area. It serves as India's major centre for arts, commerce, education, tourism, and transit, and has been the capital of several empires for over 2,000 years, featuring well-preserved historic sites from Muslim and British rule between 1193 and 1947.

Delhi experiences a dry winter humid subtropical climate (Köppen Cwa), with hot summers up to 47°C, cold winters down to -0.6°C, and annual rainfall of 774.4 mm, peaking in July-August. Air pollution is severe, especially October-December, with PM2.5 levels often exceeding safe limits; masks recommended during winter.

subsection: Districts - New Delhi and Old Delhi
text: Delhi divides into districts for travel: New Delhi (Chanakyapuri, Central Vista, Connaught Place, Pragati Maidan, Purana Qila) – British-built capital with wide boulevards, colonial buildings, monuments. Old Delhi (Ajmeri Gate, Chandni Chowk, Daryaganj, Kashmere Gate, Red Fort) – historic core with Red Fort and Jama Masjid.

Central Delhi (Civil Lines, Feroz Shah Kotla, Karol Bagh, Paharganj, Raj Ghat) – budget hotels, medieval Firozabad, British Civil Lines. South Delhi (Hauz Khas, Mehrauli, Saket, Siri Fort, Vasant Kunj, Vasant Vihar) – upscale areas, Qutub Minar, malls. South East Delhi (CR Park, Greater Kailash, Humayun's Tomb, Kalkaji, Lajpat Nagar, Okhla, Nizamuddin, Shaheen Bagh, Sunder Nursery, Tughlakabad) – high-income, historic tombs. East Delhi (Akshardham, Shahdara), West Delhi (Dwarka, Janakpuri, Punjabi Bagh).

Airports: Indira Gandhi International (DEL T1 domestic/T3 international), Safdarjung (helicopters). Airport Metro Express (Orange Line) connects T3 to New Delhi station in ~20 min. Taxis from airport: use prepaid booths to avoid scams (₹400-700 to central Delhi). App-based Uber/Ola also available from airport.

Major railway stations: New Delhi (NDLS) – main hub, Old Delhi (DLI) – serves north/east routes, Hazrat Nizamuddin (NZM) – south/central India trains, Anand Vihar (ANVT) – UP/Bihar routes. All stations connected to Delhi Metro. Book tickets via IRCTC online. Rajdhani/Shatabdi expresses connect major cities.

Delhi Metro: 12 lines (Red, Yellow, Blue 1-3, Green 1-2, Violet 1-2, Orange Airport Express, Pink 1-2, Magenta 1-2, Silver); fares ₹10-60, tourist Smart Card ₹150/day, women-only coach. DTC buses (red/green routes ₹5-25, cluster AC electric); crowded. Airport Metro Express (Orange Line) to New Delhi station.

Taxis: black-yellow (meter ₹200-500/trip), app-based Uber/Ola/Rapido (₹20/km). Autos (CNG three-wheelers ₹30-150 short trips). Cycle rickshaws in Old Delhi, e-rickshaws short distances. HOHO hop-on-hop-off bus tours covering monuments and museums (₹600/2 days). Late-night metro ends ~11 PM; use taxis/Ola/Uber after.

Walk Connaught Place (shopping, central park), explore Old Delhi narrow galis (lanes) by cycle rickshaw (Chandni Chowk, Kinari Bazaar), stroll Rashtrapati Bhavan to India Gate along Kartavya Path (3-4 km). Hop-on-hop-off HOHO bus tours covering monuments and museums (₹600/2 days). Sanjay Van forest walks in South Delhi (group recommended, daylight only).

Red Fort (Lal Qila) is a 17th-century Mughal palace fortress in Old Delhi, built by Shah Jahan. UNESCO World Heritage Site occupying 254 acres with red sandstone walls, Diwan-i-Aam, Diwan-i-Khas (hall of private audience), Rang Mahal. Entry ₹600 foreigners/₹50 Indians; open sunrise-sunset, closed Mondays. Evening sound & light show Jai Hind brings history alive. Sound & light ₹60, Hindi 7:30PM, English 8:30PM Oct-Mar.

India Gate is a 42m war memorial arch in New Delhi along Kartavya Path, commemorating 82,000 Indian & British soldiers killed in WW1 & Afghan wars. Designed by Edwin Lutyens. Amar Jawan Jyoti eternal flame for 1971 war soldiers underneath. Stunning night illumination with fountain lights; surrounding lawns popular picnic spot. Free entry, open all days.

Qutub Minar is 73m tallest brick minaret, 13th-century victory tower by Qutb-ud-din Aibak & Iltutmish. Intricate Quranic carvings, iron pillar (never rusts). UNESCO site in Mehrauli Archaeological Park with Quwwat-ul-Islam Mosque ruins. Entry ₹600 foreigners/₹40 Indians; open sunrise-sunset, closed Fridays. Light & sound show evenings.

Lotus Temple is Bahá'í House of Worship shaped like 27-petalled lotus flower, white marble in South Delhi. Architect Fariborz Sahba. Symbolizes unity of all religions. Free entry, open Tuesdays-Sundays 9AM-5PM (summers), 9AM-7PM (winters), closed Mondays. Serene meditation hall seats 2500; nine surrounding pools. No photos inside.

Humayun's Tomb (16th century) first garden-tomb of Mughal emperor Humayun, precursor to Taj Mahal design. UNESCO site in Nizamuddin East with symmetrical gardens, high domes, red sandstone & white marble. Also tombs of Bega Begum, Isa Khan. Entry ₹600 foreigners/₹30 Indians; open sunrise-sunset, closed Fridays.

Akshardham Temple massive modern Hindu complex on Yamuna river with pink sandstone & marble carvings, golden statue of Swaminarayan. Musical fountain show, boat ride through Indian culture, exhibitions. Entry free, exhibitions ₹170, shows ₹80-300. Open Tue-Sun 10AM-6:30PM, closed Mondays/public holidays. No photography inside exhibitions.

Jama Masjid India's largest mosque (17th century) by Shah Jahan, Old Delhi. Red sandstone & white marble, 25,000 prayer capacity, two 40m minarets. Views over Chandni Chowk from minaret. Entry ₹50 camera, ₹200 video; minaret ₹100. Open 7AM-sunset, non-Muslims after 12:30PM.

Raj Ghat black marble memorial platform where Mahatma Gandhi cremated 1948. Simple black stone, eternal flame, surrounding gardens, prayer meetings Fridays 5PM. Nearby memorials: Shanti Van (Jawaharlal Nehru), Shakti Sthal (Indira Gandhi). Free entry, open sunrise-sunset.

Walk Connaught Place (shopping, central park), explore Old Delhi narrow galis (lanes) by cycle rickshaw (Chandni Chowk, Kinari Bazaar), stroll Rashtrapati Bhavan to India Gate along Kartavya Path (3-4 km). Hop-on-hop-off HOHO bus tours covering monuments and museums (₹600/2 days). Sanjay Van forest walks in South Delhi (group recommended, daylight only).

Chandni Chowk Old Delhi's main market since 17th century, divided into Kinari Bazaar (wedding), Dariba Kalan (jewellery), Moti Bazaar (bridal clothes). Famous street food: Paranthe Wali Gali stuffed parathas, jalebi, chaat stalls. Cycle rickshaw best navigation through narrow lanes. Metro Chandni Chowk/Ramakrishna Ashram.

Hauz Khas Village South Delhi artsy area around 14th-century reservoir ruins. Cafes, pubs, boutiques, galleries in medieval setting. Sunset views from lakeside. Popular youth nightlife spot. Metro Green Park.

Connaught Place (CP) colonial-era shopping circle New Delhi, white colonnaded Georgian architecture. State emporiums (handicrafts), Imperial Hotel, underground market, cinemas. Central Park events. Metro Rajiv Chowk (biggest interchange).

Paharganj 'backpacker central' near New Delhi Railway Station. Budget hotels ₹500-2000, rooftop restaurants, Main Bazaar clothes/jewellery/drugs. Chaos but authentic Delhi. Metro New Delhi/Ramakrishna Ashram.

Paranthe Wali Gali narrow lane in Chandni Chowk famous for stuffed parathas (potato, paneer, rabri sweet). Historic shops like Pt. Gaya Prasad Shivkishan, Paranthe Wali Gali (150+ years), Kanwarji. ₹50-150/plate. Crowded lunch hours; vegetarian only.

Nightlife hubs: Connaught Place (CP) bars/clubs like My Bar Headquarters, The Vault. Hauz Khas Village (South Delhi) artsy pubs, live music, galleries. Nehru Place, Saket malls (PVR complexes). Greater Kailash/CR Park pubs, upscale Vasant Kunj/Vasant Vihar. Late-night metro ends ~11 PM; use taxis/Ola/Uber. Last call ~1 AM.

Budget: Paharganj (backpacker guesthouses ₹500-1500/night near New Delhi station), Karol Bagh. Mid-range: Central Delhi hotels, South Delhi (Hauz Khas, Greater Kailash). Upscale: New Delhi near Connaught Place/Khan Market, Vasant Vihar/Vasant Kunj guest houses, airport hotels. Heritage stays in Old/South Delhi havelis.

Delhi chaotic with hazardous traffic; pedestrians beware. Common scams: overpriced taxis from airport/train stations (use prepaid booths), fake tourist offices/guides, gem/tailor scams. Women: avoid night travel alone, modest dress, use ladies-only metro coaches. Pollution extreme winter (masks/N95 essential). Safe areas: South Delhi, CP; tourist police at monuments.

Air pollution is severe, especially October-December, with PM2.5 levels often exceeding safe limits; masks/N95 recommended during winter months. Summers reach up to 47°C – carry water, avoid midday outdoor exertion. Drink only bottled/filtered water. Stomach issues common; eat at hygienic establishments. Nearest hospitals: AIIMS (South Delhi), RML Hospital (Central Delhi), Apollo (South Delhi).

Agra 200km south, Taj Mahal (UNESCO), Agra Fort, Fatehpur Sikri. Gatimaan Express 4hrs (₹1500 AC Chair Car), Yamuna Expressway drive 3hrs, buses 5-6hrs. Best day/overnight trip combo Golden Triangle (Delhi-Agra-Jaipur).
Jaipur 260km southwest, Pink City, Amber Fort, Hawa Mahal, City Palace. Shatabdi Express 5hrs (₹700 AC Chair), overnight buses 5-6hrs, drive 5hrs NH48. Classic Golden Triangle leg.
Rishikesh 240km north Ganges, yoga capital, rafting, Beatles Ashram, Laxman Jhula. Overnight buses 6hrs (₹500-800 Volvo), trains Haridwar+Rishikesh shuttle 30min. Weekend spiritual/adventure getaway.
